checks.flowerCount_one: The owner expected to see {{count}} flower planted. But you planted {{got}}.
checks.flowerCount_other: The owner expected to see {{count}} flowers planted. But you planted {{got}}.
checks.flowerAtPosition: Expected a flower at position {{position}}, but didn't find one.
checks.strayFlower: Found a flower at position {{position}}, which isn't where the owner wanted one. The flowers should be evenly spaced.

plantFlowersEvenly.name: Plant flowers evenly
plantFlowersEvenly.description: Use askNumberOfFlowers() to find out how many flowers to plant, calculate the gap, and plant them evenly spaced.

oneFlower.name: The owner wants 1 flower
oneFlower.description: In this scenario, `askNumberOfFlowers()` will return 1. Plant 1 flower at position 50.

threeFlowers.name: The owner wants 3 flowers
threeFlowers.description: In this scenario, `askNumberOfFlowers()` will return 3. Plant 3 flowers at positions 25, 50, and 75.

fourFlowers.name: The owner wants 4 flowers
fourFlowers.description: In this scenario, `askNumberOfFlowers()` will return 4. Plant 4 flowers at positions 20, 40, 60, and 80.

nineFlowers.name: The owner wants 9 flowers
nineFlowers.description: In this scenario, `askNumberOfFlowers()` will return 9. Plant 9 flowers at positions 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, and 90.

chooseGap.question: How do I work out where to plant each flower?
chooseGap.answer:
First work out the gap between flowers:

```javascript
100 / (numberOfFlowers + 1)
```

The first flower goes at that position, and each next flower is one more gap along. So if there are 3 flowers the gap is 25, and they go at 25, 50 and 75.

seeScenarios.question: How do I see the different scenarios
seeScenarios.answer: Click on the circles on the left hand side in between your code and the results.

askNumberOfFlowers.description: Returns the **number of flowers** to plant.

plant.description: Plants a flower at the given **position**.
